purpose: Conceptual overview of how Synapse integrates with any target project. Explains the standalone, project-agnostic architecture with two-path system ({tracker_root} vs {project_root}), what lives where, integration steps (point Synapse at project, read context, execute in project), three-tier project root resolution priority, what Synapse creates in the project (.synapse/ directory only), multi-project support across 5 dashboards, and portability guarantees (zero npm deps, no hardcoded paths, offline-capable).
gotchas: Synapse contains ZERO hardcoded paths -- everything uses placeholders
Workers receive BOTH paths in dispatch prompts; code goes to {project_root}, progress to {tracker_root}
Each dashboard can target a different project via task.project_root in initialization.json
Synapse warns (does not block) if directory lacks .git/ or recognizable project markers
patterns: Two-path separation: orchestration data at {tracker_root}, code work at {project_root}
Resolution priority chain: --project flag > stored config > CWD
Per-swarm project tracking via initialization.json task.project_root
conventions: Only .synapse/ written to project; everything else stays at tracker_root
